title: hypericum ovalifolium subsp. hisauchii
intro: Hypericum ovalifolium subsp. hisauchii, also known as Japanese Tutsan, is a subspecies of the St. John's Wort plant (Hypericum species), characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its hypericin and hyperforin content. The health benefits of Japanese Tutsan include its potential to reduce anxiety and depression, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy. Japanese Tutsan can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used internally or applied topically to the skin for its medicinal benefits.
